content:
教育和工作经历 # 香港教育大学，博士在读 (2025-预期2028)
香港教育大学，研究助理 (2023-2025)
清华大学，研究助理 (2022)
华南师范大学，教育学硕士，基础心理学专业 (2019-2022) 江西师范大学，理学学士，心理学 (师范) 专业 (2015-2019) 研究兴趣 # 读写发展及相关障碍 阅读理解 学习与指导 技术增强学习 代表性论文 # *：共同第一作者；#：通讯作者
Li, Q*., Wei, Z*#., Xu, Z., Zhang, S., & Liu, D#. (Accepted). Psychological and ecological factors predict reading interest in Chinese primary school children: A two-year investigation. Reading and Writing.
Wei, Z., Yin, S., Clariana, R. B#., & Chen, X#. (2025). The influence of pre-reading purpose and extra-textual networks with summary writing on multiple document concept network integration: A replication of Wei et al. (2024). Educational Technology Research and Development, Online First. [Link] Wei, Z., Wang, L., Xu, Z., Luo, L., Zhang, X., Li, N., & Liu, D#. (2024). The global and local pattern of reading-related cognitive and ecological factors in Chinese first-grade children: A cross-sectional network analysis. Journal of Learning Disabilities, Online First. [Link] Wei, Z., Zhang, Y., Clariana, R. B#., & Chen, X#. (2024). The effects of reading prompts and post-reading generative learning tasks on multiple document integration: Evidence from concept network analysis. Educational Technology Research and Development, 72, 661–685. [Link]

title: 个人简历

content:
Check my Google Scholar page ! # *: Contributed equally; #: Corresponding author
Li, Q*., Wei, Z*., Xu, Z., Zhang, S., & Liu, D#. (Accepted). Psychological and ecological factors predict reading interest in Chinese primary school children: A two-year investigation. Reading and Writing. Wang, J*., He, Y*., Liu, X., Li, X., Wei, Z., Du, X., Wang, Y., Lei, T., Zhu, D., & Zhou, X#. (2025). Stay in unhappy marriage relates to children’s psychopathology:a large-scale cross-sectional study. Journal of Affective Disorders, available online. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jad.2025.119725 Wei, Z., Yin, S., Clariana, R. B#., & Chen, X#. (2025). The influence of pre-reading purpose and extra-textual networks with summary writing on multiple document concept network integration: A replication of Wei et al. (2024). Educational Technology Research and Development, early acess online. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11423-025-10508-8 Wei, Z., Wang, L., Xu, Z., Luo, L., Zhang, X., Li, N., & Liu, D#. (2024). The global and local pattern of reading-related cognitive and ecological factors in Chinese first-grade children: A cross-sectional network analysis. Journal of Learning Disabilities, available online. https://doi.org/10.1177/00222194241283198 Jia, L*., Wei, Z*., Liu, B*., Yu, M., Zhang, X., He, X., Xi, Y., Chen, R#., & Zhang, X#. (2024). The developmental and dynamic relationship between psychopathology and psychotic-like 1 experiences trajectories in children and adolescents. Journal of Affective Disorders, available online. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jad.2024.09.100 Wei, Z., Zhang, Y., Clariana, R. B#., & Chen, X#. (2024). The effects of reading prompts and post-reading generative learning tasks on multiple document integration: Evidence from concept network analysis. Educational Technology Research and Development, 72, 661–685. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11423-023-10326-w Jia, L*., Wei, Z*., Zhang, X., Wen, X., Wang, J., Zhang, X#., & Chen, R#. (2024). The associations between baseline characteristics and different developmental trajectories of psychotic-like experiences in children and adolescents. Brain Research, 1832, 148853. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.brainres.2024.148853 Wang, J*., Wei, Z*., Jia, L*., Yu, J., Wang, Y., Meng, Y., Feng, Y#., & Chen, R#. (2024). Can digital self-harm beyond traditional non-suicidal self-injuries predict suicide risk? Acta Psychiatrica Scandinavica, 149(3), 256-266. http://doi.org/10.1111/acps.13658 Qu, D*., Liu, D*., Cai, C*., Zhang, X., Yu, J., Zhang, Q., Liu, K., Wei, Z., Tan, J., Cui, Z., Zhang, X#., & Chen, R#. (2023). Process model of emotion regulation-based digital intervention for emotional problems. Digit Health, 9. https://doi.org/10.1177/20552076231187476 Chen, X., Wei, Z., Li, Z., & Clariana, R. B#. (2023). The influence of the conceptual structure of external representations when relearning history content. Educational Technology Research and Development, 71, 415–439. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11423-022-10176-y Wei, Z., Chen, X#., & Clariana, R., B. (2022). Measures of knowledge structure in reading comprehension. Journal of Psychological Science, 45(2), 306-315. [in Chinese] https://doi.org/10.16719/j.cnki.1671-6981.20220206 Manuscripts submitted
Liu, D*#., Wei, Z*., Maurer, U., Chung, K., Datu, J., Fern-Pollak, L., Wydell, T., Tso, W., Sun, F., Luo, L., Wang, L-C., Yum, Y., Xu, G., & Li, S. (Submitted). Profiling Chinese children with symptoms of SpLD, ADHD, or ASD: A transdiagnostic and biopsychosocial study. Wei, Z., Wang, L., Xu, Z., Zhang, X., Li, N., Wang, L-C., Liu, S., Sun, F., Zhao, J., & Liu, D#. (Under review). The profile of Chinese children with reading difficulties: Do non-cognitive factors matter? Manuscripts in preparation
Using cross-sectional and cross-lagged networks to reveal the interplays of reading-related cognitive, psychological, and ecological factors in Chinese primary school children. Cognitive and psychological factors in Chinese primary school students with reading difficulties, mathematics difficulies, and the comorbid condition. Conference presentation (Only those in which I was the presenter/reporter are listed)
Wei, Z., Liu, D#. (Nov, 2024). Cognitive and psychological factors in Chinese primary school students with reading difficulties, mathematics difficulties, and the comorbid condition. [Oral presentation in a collaborative symposium]. The First Global Heads Forum in Education and The Inauguration Conference of The Global Alliance of Educational Leaders and Department Heads (GAELDH). Hong Kong: InnoCentre. Wei, Z., Zhang, Y., Yin, S., Chen, X#., Clariana, R. B. (Nov, 2022). Richer external cues before reading, better integration of multiple documents maintaining in memory: Evidence from concept network analysis [Oral presentation]. The 24th Chinese Congress of Psychology. China: Henan Normal University.
